perf.regime: CUTE DSL fp32 online-softmax merge on Grace+Blackwell GB10 (sm_121). Memory-bound: one CTA per (t,h) row (n_rows = T*H), 128 threads tile the head dim D (thread-stride, coalesced — out_a[t,h,:] is contiguous in D). Per-row weights (wa=exp(lse_a-m), wb=exp(lse_b-m), denom, m) are scalar, so each thread computes them locally (3 transcendentals, hidden behind the memory-bound D reads — no SMEM broadcast needed). Thread 0 writes the scalar lse[row]=m+log(denom); every thread writes its own out[row,d]. Host upcasts out_a/out_b to fp32 (bit-identical to the reference's out_a.float()) and casts out back to out_a.dtype (lse stays fp32). Uses natural exp/log (math.exp/math.log) matching the reference.

measured.source: scripts/ds5_merge_perf.py + ncu on ds5 GB10 sm_121, CTK 13.0, post compile-cache. END-TO-END incl launch.

notes:
CUTE DSL (`cutlass.cute`, nvidia-cutlass-dsl 4.5.2) backend for mha_merge_state; fourth native-CUDA card in the library, validated on the ds5 Grace+Blackwell GB10 test bed (docs/ds5-cute-testbed.md). JIT-compiles via nvcc (CUDA_HOME must be set). Registers only if nvidia-cutlass-dsl imports; else degrades to the triton/reference card.

Design (src/xkernels/ops/attention/cute/merge_state_kernel.py): the [T,H,D] tensors are flattened to [n_rows, D] and [T,H] to [n_rows] (row = t*H+h). One CTA per row, threads tile D. The per-row softmax weights are scalar, computed locally per thread (cheap, memory-bound hides it). Pure-fp32 device kernel (host does bf16<->fp32 plumbing). Natural exp/log (math.exp/math.log) — matches the reference exactly (the triton card uses log2+/LOG2E as an equivalent optimization).

Compile-cache: per-(n_rows,D) cute.compile handle (tensors-only launch), same pattern as mm_fp8_blockscale.cuda. Device guard raises RuntimeError on CPU tensors so verify_parity (device='cpu') records CUDA as a caught backend error instead of segfaulting.
